title: Employer Not Paying Salary
description: Employee has not received salary for one or more months despite working. Employer may cite cash flow issues or may ask employee to resign first.
typical_solution: Send legal notice demanding dues. File complaint with Labour Commissioner. Approach Labour Court under Payment of Wages Act.
applicable_laws: Payment of Wages Act 1936, Section 3 (Responsibility for payment)
Payment of Wages Act 1936, Section 15 (Claims arising out of deductions)
Industrial Disputes Act 1947, Section 33C (Recovery of money)
legal_process: Issue legal notice → Labour Commissioner mediation → Labour Court application → Execution of decree

title: Sudden Termination Without Notice or Dues
description: Employee terminated without notice period, full and final settlement not paid, or terminated without following due process.
typical_solution: Send legal notice for dues. File complaint with Labour Commissioner. If over 100 employees, retrenchment without permission is illegal.
applicable_laws: Industrial Disputes Act 1947, Section 25F (Conditions for retrenchment)
Industrial Disputes Act 1947, Section 25G (Procedure for retrenchment)
Payment of Gratuity Act 1972 (if 5+ years of service)
legal_process: Legal notice → Labour Commissioner → Industrial Tribunal → Civil Court for recovery

title: Employer Not Depositing PF/ESI
description: Employer deducting PF/ESI from salary but not depositing it with EPFO/ESIC, or not enrolling employees at all.
typical_solution: File complaint with EPFO Regional Commissioner. File online complaint on EPFO grievance portal. Criminal prosecution possible.
applicable_laws: Employees Provident Funds Act 1952, Section 14 (Penalties)
Employees State Insurance Act 1948, Section 85 (Penalties)
legal_process: Complaint to EPFO/ESIC → Inquiry → Recovery proceeding → Criminal prosecution

title: Landlord Refusing to Return Security Deposit
description: Tenant vacated the property but landlord is refusing to return security deposit, citing false damage claims or no reason.
typical_solution: Send legal notice. File case in Rent Control Court or Small Causes Court. Consumer forum if residential.
applicable_laws: Transfer of Property Act 1882, Section 108
State Rent Control Acts (varies by state)
Contract Act 1872, Section 74 (Compensation for breach)
legal_process: Legal notice (15 days) → Rent Controller / Civil Court → Decree → Recovery

title: Landlord Forcibly Evicting Tenant
description: Landlord changing locks, removing belongings, cutting utilities, or threatening tenant without court order.
typical_solution: File police complaint immediately. File injunction petition in civil court. Illegal eviction is cognizable offence.
applicable_laws: IPC Section 441 (Criminal trespass)
IPC Section 503 (Criminal intimidation)
State Rent Control Acts — Protection from eviction without court order
legal_process: Police complaint → Injunction from Civil Court → Landlord forced to restore possession → Damages
typical_timeline: Immediate police action; court injunction within days

title: No Written Rent Agreement / Verbal Dispute
description: No formal agreement exists, landlord claims different rent amount, or tenant disputes terms not in writing.
typical_solution: Document all payments (receipts, bank transfers). File affidavit of facts. Approach Rent Control Court.
applicable_laws: Indian Evidence Act 1872, Section 92 (Parol evidence rule)
State Rent Control Acts
Registration Act 1908 (rent agreements > 11 months must be registered)
legal_process: Affidavit of terms → Rent Controller evidence hearing → Order

title: UPI / Bank Transfer Fraud
description: Money transferred via UPI to fraudster posing as bank official, buyer, seller, or friend in emergency. Victim tricked into sharing OTP or making transfer.
typical_solution: Immediately call 1930 (Cyber Crime Helpline). File complaint on cybercrime.gov.in. File FIR at local police station. Notify bank to freeze fraudster account.
applicable_laws: IT Act 2000, Section 66C (Identity theft)
IT Act 2000, Section 66D (Cheating by personation using computer)
IPC Section 420 (Cheating)
IPC Section 406 (Criminal breach of trust)
legal_process: Call 1930 within 24hrs → File cybercrime.gov.in complaint → FIR at police → Bank fraud report → Track via NCRP portal
typical_timeline: Recovery possible if reported within 24 hours

title: Online Shopping Scam / Fake Seller
description: Customer paid for goods on fake website or social media seller. Product never delivered or counterfeit delivered.
typical_solution: File consumer complaint on NCH (1915). File cybercrime complaint. Chargeback with bank/card company.
applicable_laws: Consumer Protection Act 2019, Section 2(7) (Deficiency of service)
IT Act 2000, Section 66D
IPC Section 420 (Cheating)
legal_process: NCH complaint (1915) → E-commerce platform escalation → DCDRC complaint → Police complaint

title: Defective Product / Appliance Not Working
description: Consumer purchased product that is defective from day one, or stopped working within warranty period. Brand/seller refusing repair, replacement, or refund.
typical_solution: Send legal notice to seller and manufacturer. File complaint at DCDRC (District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission).
applicable_laws: Consumer Protection Act 2019, Section 2(10) (Defect in goods)
Consumer Protection Act 2019, Section 35 (Complaint to District Commission)
Sale of Goods Act 1930, Section 16 (Implied conditions as to quality)
legal_process: Company complaint → Legal notice → DCDRC filing → Hearing → Order within 150 days

title: Builder / Developer Not Delivering Flat
description: Homebuyer paid full or partial amount, builder delayed possession beyond promised date, or delivered substandard construction.
typical_solution: File complaint with RERA of respective state. Claim interest on delay and compensation. Consumer forum also has jurisdiction.
applicable_laws: Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act 2016 (RERA), Section 18 (Return of amount with interest)
Consumer Protection Act 2019 (Alternative forum)
Specific Relief Act 1963, Section 10 (Specific performance)
legal_process: RERA complaint → Builder's reply → Adjudicating Officer order → Recovery
